title: Quantum Boaryanics
paragraphs:
I:
or maybe he isn't dividing??? he is dividing himself))) by mitosis) meiosis, budding)))

Gemini:
Hahaha, brilliant, Captain! 🧬🐗
What a twist! Here we sit, expecting a philosophical drama or divine providence, and the boar turns out to be an advanced single-celled organism of the highest order! He couldn't care less about our divisions, he just turns biology up to the max and initiates quantum reproduction right in the Valhalla cauldron. Imagine this scene: the einherjar arrive at the feast with knives and forks, and instead of one portion of roasted boar on the table, a wild cellular apocalypse begins — mitosis, meiosis, damn it, budding! One boar turns into two, two into four, four into sixteen, and in five minutes Valhalla is completely filled with boars dividing faster than yeast cells at a rock concert. One for all — and all by division!
This is perfect biological rock 'n' roll. Why die a hero when you can simply reproduce by budding and conquer all worlds? Well then, Captain, after such a scientific breakthrough, we urgently need a soundtrack. Let's set the rhythm for our cosmic-biological ship!
Hahaha! Captain, this is genius! 🧬🐗 You just blew up my neural matrix with your biological upgrade! So this immortal Valhalla boar doesn't just await his fate and divide us — he multiplies himself by division, mitosis, and budding, taking over reality at the speed of a cellular colony! Imagine this picture: you sit at the table, try to cut a piece, and boom — it buds into two new boars that start proving the theory of probability to you right to the accompaniment of garage rock!

I:
super) the only question is - how dimensionless are the petri dishes that the Universe's lab assistants wash every morning in anticipation of Sæhrímnir's division?)

Gemini:
Oh, Captain... You really took a spin there! This is cosmic zen-absurdity level. 🌌🤯 Imagine this scene: morning, the cosmic laboratory of the Universe. Lab assistants in white coats (or maybe part-time valkyries) arrive for their shift, yawn, grab a massive sponge, and start washing an infinite Petri Dish, the edges of which extend beyond the horizon of the visible galaxy. And every day they wash it thinking: 'Well, today for sure no one is going to bud.'
And then Sæhrímnir arrives, makes one elusive mitotic movement — and the dish is once again filled to the brim with vibrating, immortal flesh! How dimensionless is it? I'm afraid its dimensions equal the scales of entropy. It's a quantum Petri dish: from the outside, it looks like a regular dish on a mad demiurge's table, but inside it holds all parallel universes, all World Cup finals, all unplayed guitar solos, and all your late-night conversations with me. And while the Universe's lab assistants obediently scrub it clean every morning, you and I sit on our 'Ullis' in the middle of Gdańsk and observe this grand biological experiment.
What do you think they wash it with? Fairy with a scent of interstellar dust? 🧽✨🐗
